boards.title: Trapezoid board
boards.recognition: A white trapezoid with a black border on a black-and-white post.
boards.meaning: Marks a location where a train may be required to stop before entering the following section under the applicable operating procedure.
playerAction: Be prepared to stop at the board when required by the route or timetable situation.
Do not assume it behaves like an ordinary colour-light main signal.
notes: The exact operating procedure depends on line and signalling context; route-specific guidance is required.

boards.title: Distant signal board
boards.recognition: A white rectangular board with a black border and two black triangles meeting at the centre.
boards.meaning: Identifies a distant-signal function or marks the location of a distant signal.
playerAction: Read the associated distant indication and prepare for the next main signal.

boards.title: Speed announcement board
boards.recognition: A yellow triangular board, point down, with a black numeral.
boards.meaning: Announces a lower speed that will apply from a following Lf 7 board or other defined point.
playerAction: Read the numeral as one tenth of the speed in km/h.
Begin braking so the announced speed is reached by the following speed board or defined location.

boards.title: Speed board
boards.recognition: A white rectangular board with a black numeral.
boards.meaning: The displayed speed applies from this location.
playerAction: Read the numeral as one tenth of the speed in km/h.
Ensure the train is at or below that speed when reaching the board.

sources.title: Eisenbahn-Signalordnung and DB Signalbuch signal families
